title: ZXBrick Overview
description: A practical introduction to ZXBrick, the core gameplay loop, and supporting systems.
summary: ZXBrick is a retro brick-breaking arcade game in the ZX Series with level progression, achievements, and shared wallet support.

sections.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ZXBrick is a retro-inspired brick-breaking game in the [ZX Series](/zx-series). It combines level progression, unlock systems, and replay-focused progression loops through achievements, leaderboards, and shared currency support.
Players typically progress through 100 handcrafted stages, build momentum through repeated sessions, and use achievement and leaderboard feedback to guide improvement over time.

sections.heading: Where to start
paragraphs: Start with How to Play, then read Controls and Game Modes before exploring power-ups and strategy pages.
Use the page links at the top of each page to jump between sections quickly.

sections.heading: How this connects to the ZX Series
paragraphs: The guide is connected to other ZX Series pages where shared progression systems are used, including [BarkinMad Coins](/zx-series).
The wider [ZX Series](/zx-series) includes related arcade titles such as ZXSnake and [ZXSpace](/apps/zxspace).

featureShowcases.heading: ZXBrick at a glance
featureShowcases.summary: Overview introduces ZXBrick as a ZX Series brick-breaking arcade game.
body: ZXBrick is a classic brick-breaking arcade game where every level challenges your reactions, accuracy, and timing. Clear every brick, collect powerful upgrades, and master increasingly difficult layouts as you progress.
Whether you’re playing for the first time or aiming for a higher score, this guide will help you understand the core mechanics and get the most out of every game.
bullets: How ZXBrick works and what makes it fun.
What you’ll find throughout the rest of the guide.
Where to learn about controls, game modes, power-ups, and progression.

image.caption: ZXBrick gameplay artwork introduces the app and its core loop.

featureShowcases.heading: Progression and support topics
featureShowcases.summary: ZXBrick guide pages connect gameplay with leaderboards, achievements, BarkinMad Coins, tips, FAQ, and screenshots.
body: This matters because ZX Series pages are not only image galleries. They should explain how the player moves from learning the game into repeated play and progress tracking.
Players use these supporting pages after the first session to understand goals, scoring surfaces, unlocks, and practical tips.
bullets: Learn how gameplay to progression and support pages.
Keeps guide flow consistent across the ZX Series.
Uses existing artwork as placeholders where page-specific screenshots are not available.

image.caption: Use this artwork to confirm your next gameplay goals before the next session.
